body:
## 🧪 Manage Tests?

Select files to manage tests for (create, update, or remove):

- [x] modified `src/App.ts`
- [x] modified `src/ClipboardCapture.ts`
- [x] modified `src/Diagnostics.ts`
- [x] modified `src/OS.ts`
- [x] modified `src/OSLinux.ts`
- [x] modified `src/OSMac.ts`
- [x] modified `src/OSWin.ts`
- [x] modified `src/PDF.ts`
- [x] modified `src/PaperPrinter.ts`
- [x] modified `src/Stylize.ts`
- [x] modified `src/TabInspector.ts`
- [x] modified `src/UI.ts`
- [x] modified `src/UIMenu.ts`
- [x] modified `src/UIMenuMgr.ts`
- [x] modified `src/VSCodeAPIs.ts`
- [x] added `src/lib/pdf.min.js`
- [x] added `src/lib/pdf.worker.min.js`

---

- [ ] Yes, manage tests

Click the checkbox and GitAuto will add/update/remove tests for the selected files to this PR.
If GitAuto's commits are not satisfactory, you can reset to your original state from your local branch:

```bash
git checkout fix-compilation-errors
git push --force-with-lease origin fix-compilation-errors
```


You can [turn off triggers](https://gitauto.ai/settings/triggers?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral), [update coding rules](https://gitauto.ai/settings/rules?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral), or [exclude files](https://gitauto.ai/dashboard/coverage?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral).
For contact, email us at [info@gitauto.ai](mailto:info@gitauto.ai) or visit [our contact page](https://gitauto.ai/contact?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral)

performed_via_github_app.name: GitAuto AI
performed_via_github_app.description:
## What we offer

[GitAuto](https://gitauto.ai?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) is an AI software engineer that writes code for you to complete more bug fixes and feature requests.

## How to install

Install GitAuto by clicking the "Configure" button in the upper right corner. Ensure you're the repository owner. Select the owner (organization or personal account) and the specific repository for GitAuto use.

## How to get started

[Here](https://youtu.be/gulhHrKCPxQ) is 1 min demo. First, create an issue, such as a bug report or feature request. Click the checkbox from GitAuto's comment to generate a PR. Then, you just need to wait for the PR is created. GitAuto updates you on progress in the issue comments. Or you can pick up an exisiting issue and trigger GitAuto by labeling `gitauto`. Here are [some case studies](https://gitauto.ai/blog?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral)

## Data policy

GitAuto will read your issues, file tree and necessary files in your repo and they are processed by OpenAI but not stored at all as we are applied to [Zero Data Retention](https://openai.com/enterprise-privacy/#2Hc3bUMJHSPrw3aDCS8hYA-button) by OpenAI.

## Pricing

Get started for free. [Subscribe](https://gitauto.ai#pricing) when you want to use it more.

For any inquiries, reach out to us at [info@gitauto.ai](mailto:info@gitauto.ai).

body:
## Walkthrough Docs overhaul details new architecture and execution flow. Core refactors introduce ordered component lifecycle, unified fileRead, URI-aware webviews, OS clipboard APIs, PDF HTML templating, Shiki v3 token types, and a new Stylize→PDF path. Multiple public signatures change. Extensive test removals; one new macOS AppleScript template test added. ## Changes | Cohort / File(s) | Summary | | --- | --- | | **Documentation**
`AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `DIAGNOSTICS_REFACTOR_PLAN.md`, `REFACTOR_TO_NAMED_PARAMS.md`, `edm-refactor-todos.md` | Expanded architecture, execution flow, diagnostics refactor plan, EDM/Shiki v3 migration, formatting standardization; no code. | | **App lifecycle and typing**
`src/App.ts` | Introduces typed component order for init/done; strengthens vscode typing; adds templateDictReplace helper; centralizes lifecycle iteration. | | **OS abstraction**
`src/OS.ts` | Requires App; adds generic fileRead(key-aware), htmlSrcPathToURI; defines abstract clipboard ops; removes readYaml/readJson helpers; refines factory selection. | | **Platform implementations**
`src/OSMac.ts`, `src/OSLinux.ts`, `src/OSWin.ts`, `src/OSMac.yaml` | Constructors now require App; adds clipboard APIs (mac implemented via AppleScript templates; win/linux stubs); fileOpenPrintDialog param rename; macOS adopts YAML AppleScript templates. | | **Clipboard pipeline**
`src/ClipboardCapture.ts`, `src/ClipboardCapture.yaml` | Replaces AppleScript with OS clipboard APIs; reads templates via fileRead; centralizes CSS via YAML placeholders; adds capture() alias. | | **PDF generation and viewer**
`src/PDF.ts`, `src/PDF.yaml`, `src/types/jspdf.d.ts`, `src/types/shiki.d.ts` | Switches to ThemedToken; maps font families to jsPDF; routes errors via UI; rewrites pdfToHTML to YAML-templated PDF.js viewer; declares jsPDF.getFontList; updates Shiki d.ts names. | | **Stylize and theming**
`src/Stylize.ts`, `src/Stylize.yaml`, `src/types/theme_t.ts` | Unifies Theme model (Shiki IDs + VS Code ThemeData); lazy highlighter; adds styleToPdf; removes styleToHtml; YAML token-to-HTML snippets; VS Code theme conversion pipeline. | | **UI and menus**
`src/UI.ts`, `src/UI.yaml`, `src/UIMenu.ts`, `src/UIMenuMgr.ts` | Adds URI-aware webview creation and toolbar injection; toolbar template + base CSS via YAML; menu loaders switch to fileRead with guards; minor logging changes. | | **VS Code API wrapper**
`src/VSCodeAPIs.ts` | Narrows updateGlobalState value type; switches theme JSON loading to fileRead; minor formatting. | | **Diagnostics import**
`src/Diagnostics.ts` | Replaces dynamic require with static UI import; comments adjusted; possible cycle timing change. | | **Tab inspection**
`src/TabInspector.ts` | inspectVisibleEditors now returns only active editor; formatting changes. | | **Config**
`package.json`, `tsconfig.json` | Pins @types/node to ^24.3.1; tsconfig target/libs downgraded to ES5, adds interop/decorators options. | | **Tests: removed**
`tests/App.test.ts`, `tests/ClipboardCapture.test.ts`, `tests/OS.test.ts`, `tests/OSLinux.test.ts`, `tests/OSMac.test.ts`, `tests/OSWin.test.ts`, `tests/PDF.test.ts`, `tests/PaperPrinter-PDF.test.ts`, `tests/PaperPrinter.test.ts`, `tests/Simple-PDF.test.ts`, `tests/Stylize-PDF.test.ts`, `tests/TabInspector.test.ts`, `tests/UI.test.ts` | Deletes broad unit/integration coverage across App, OS, PDF, Stylize, PaperPrinter, UI, TabInspector, etc. | | **Tests: updated/added**
`tests/UIMenuMgr.test.ts`, `tests/VSCodeAPIs.test.ts`, `tests/OSMac-AppleScript.test.ts` | Align mocks to fileRead; expand VSCode mocks and constructor surface; add AppleScript template substitution tests. | ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram autonumber actor User participant VSCode as VS Code Command System participant VSCodeAPIs participant PaperPrinter participant TabInspector participant Stylize participant PDF participant UI participant Webview as Webview (PDF.js) participant OS User->>VSCode: Trigger command p2p4vsc.print2paper VSCode->>VSCodeAPIs: executeCommand(...) VSCodeAPIs->>PaperPrinter: handleFirstPrintCommand() PaperPrinter->>TabInspector: inspectTab() TabInspector-->>PaperPrinter: { code, language, fileName } PaperPrinter->>Stylize: styleToPdf(code, language, opts) Stylize->>PDF: generatePdfFromTokens(tokens, font, size, lineHeight, title) PDF-->>Stylize: jsPDF doc Stylize-->>PaperPrinter: jsPDF doc PaperPrinter->>PDF: pdfToHTML(doc) PDF-->>PaperPrinter: HTML (templated PDF.js viewer) PaperPrinter->>UI: addToolbarWithURIs(html, panel) UI->>UI: htmlToWebViewPanelWithURIs(title, html) UI-->>PaperPrinter: WebviewPanel PaperPrinter-->>User: Preview shown rect rgba(200,230,255,0.2) note over Webview,UI: Bidirectional messaging (toolbar actions) Webview->>UI: postMessage(action) UI->>PaperPrinter: handlePrintMessage(action) PaperPrinter->>PDF: save/print actions PDF->>OS: fileOpenPrintDialog/print/direct OS-->>PDF: result end ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 5 (Critical) | ⏱️ ~120–180 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode#5 — Implements jsPDF/token-driven PDF flow; overlaps with PDF.generatePdfFromTokens, Stylize.styleToPdf, and PaperPrinter pipeline changes. - appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode#1 — Shiki v3/EDM migration and theming refactor; aligns with Stylize ThemeData model, YAML templates, and App/VSCode initialization shifts. - appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode#2 — Introduces Diagnostics; related to Diagnostics import timing change and widespread dx usage. Next. ## Pre-merge checks (3 passed)
✅ Passed checks (3 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :----------------: | :------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title Check | ✅ Passed | Title accurately and succinctly summarizes the two primary changes—fixing the PDF worker URI conversion and refactoring App component management—which align with the PR objectives and modified files (e.g., src/OS.ts, src/App.ts, added PDF worker asset). It is specific, clear, and suitable for quick scanning by reviewers. | | Docstring Coverage | ✅ Passed | No functions found in the changes. Docstring coverage check skipped. |
> [!TIP] >
> 👮 Agentic pre-merge checks are now available in preview! > > Pro plan users can now enable pre-merge checks in their settings to enforce checklists before merging PRs. > > - Built-in checks – Quickly apply ready-made checks to enforce title conventions, require pull request descriptions that follow templates, validate linked issues for compliance, and more. > - Custom agentic checks – Define your own rules using CodeRabbit’s advanced agentic capabilities to enforce organization-specific policies and workflows. For example, you can instruct CodeRabbit’s agent to verify that API documentation is updated whenever API schema files are modified in a PR. Note: Upto 5 custom checks are currently allowed during the preview period. Pricing for this feature will be announced in a few weeks. > > Please see the [documentation](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/pr-reviews/pre-merge-checks) for more information. > > Example: > > ```yaml > reviews: > pre_merge_checks: > custom_checks: > - name: "Undocumented Breaking Changes" > mode: "warning" > instructions: | > Pass/fail criteria: All breaking changes to public APIs, CLI flags, environment variables, configuration keys, database schemas, or HTTP/GraphQL endpoints must be documented in the "Breaking Change" section of the PR description and in CHANGELOG.md. Exclude purely internal or private changes (e.g., code not exported from package entry points or explicitly marked as internal). > ``` > > Please share your feedback with us on this [Discord post](https://discord.com/channels/1134356397673414807/1414771631775158383). > >
✨ Finishing touches - [ ] 📝 Generate Docstrings
🧪 Generate unit tests - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Post copyable unit tests in a comment - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `fix-compilation-errors`
--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands and usage tips.

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues
